rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: ontinued to 5/2/2023. Please file/submit all necessary documents seven days prior to the hearing. (1) Notice of Hearing must be served 30-days on all persons who are entitled. PC 17203. CRC 7.51. There has been no notice of hearing on file since the petition was filed. (2) Petition to set forth all persons entitled to notice of the petition. (3) Written consent to act from the nominated successor trustee to be filed and proper notice of hearing to be given to the nominee. (4) Proposed Order must be submitted prior to hearing. (5) Please clarify if petition seeks delay in the appointment as stated in the document filed 2/1/2023. Petition needs to update its prayer to include all requests being sought.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEGACY ENHANCEMENT POOLED MINOR'S TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Directing Establishment Of Pooled Minor'S Trust; Authorization Of Trustee'S Fees; And Approving Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues raised by the judicial officer after review and posted as the tentative ruling for the 12/06/2022 hearing: 1) Why is venue proper in San Francisco County, rather than Alameda County? The event that gave rise to the minor's claim occurred in Alameda County; the minor and Kaiser are both residents of Alameda County; the proposed trustee is a Texas corporation with its representative located in Pennsylvania; and there is no property of the proposed sub-account located in San Francisco County. If the Court is to approve this petition, the proposed sub-account with the pooled trust will be subject to continuing jurisdiction and supervision of the Court for many years until the minor attains the age of majority. 2) If the Court is to approve the proposed joinder and sub-account with the non-special needs pooled trust as prayed by this petition, what does the petitioner intend to do with Dept. 514's minor's compromise order directing distribution of partial settlement funds to a special needs pooled trust for the minor? 3) What is the initial amount of cash or other assets funding the proposed sub-account? 4) Why should the proposed joinder agreement and master pooled trust be governed by Texas law, rather than California law, even though the situs of the trust will be California, as so stated in the first amendment to the master pooled trust? An appearance may be required on 03/21/2023 on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar if no satisfactory progress is shown. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JERRY STEVEN PUCCIO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/09/2023. Please supplement the following information seven days prior. 1. Notice of remote appearance must be given to all persons entitled to. LRSF 14.1.D.(6). The heirs were noticed; however, notice of directions for remote appearances not provided. 2. Proposed Order must include a copy of the lost will that is offered to be admitted to probate. PC 8223. 3. Need clarification whether PC 6124 presumption is applicable or not. The counsel's amended declaration does not clarify how the presumption under PC 6124 is applicable. A verified supplemental declaration must address each element. (1) if the testator's will was last in the testator's possession (2)if the testator was competent until death, and (3) the will can be found after the testator's death. 4. If the presumption is applicable, a verified supplemental declaration is required to overcome the presumption. In doing so, statement of hearsays appears insufficient. For example, paragraph #6 in the amended declaration filed 3/8/2023 must be given by the petitioner's verified statement. 5. Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number must be filed. LRSF 14.18.E. This document must be filed conventionally. LRSF 14.59.C.
